section.heading: Fraudulent statement or failure to furnish statement to employee

content.p: In addition to the criminal penalty provided by section 7204, any person required under the provisions of section 6051 or 6053(b) to furnish a statement to an employee who willfully furnishes a false or fraudulent statement, or who willfully fails to furnish a statement in the manner, at the time, and showing the information required under section 6051 or 6053(b), or regulations prescribed thereunder, shall for each such failure be subject to a penalty under this subchapter of $50, which shall be assessed and collected in the same manner as the tax on employers imposed by section 3111.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 1965—Pub. L. 89–97 substituted “6051 or 6053(b)” for “6051” wherever appearing.

note.heading: Effective Date of 1965 Amendment
note.p: Amendment by section 313 of Pub. L. 89–97 applicable only with respect to tips received by employees after 1965, see section 313(f) of Pub. L. 89–97, set out as a note under section 6053 of this title.
